prompt: The same weakness — Information Exposure (CWE-200) — is found in two places in an internet-facing app. Which context makes it HIGHER severity?

options.text: When the affected component handles sensitive data or is reachable by unauthenticated, internet-facing users
When it is only reachable after authenticating as the data's owner
When a tested WAF rule already blocks the known payloads
When it sits behind a VPN that requires MFA

explanation: Severity is not a fixed property of a CWE — it depends on context. The SAME Information Exposure (CWE-200) is more severe when it touches sensitive data or is reachable by unauthenticated users; it is less severe behind strong compensating controls (VPN+MFA, an effective WAF rule) or when only reachable by the legitimate owner. This is exactly why CVSS has exploitability and impact dimensions rather than a single per-CWE number.
source_reference: FIRST CVSS v3.1 Specification; OWASP Risk Rating Methodology

prompt: The same weakness — Open Redirect (CWE-601) — is found in two places in an internet-facing app. Which context makes it HIGHER severity?

options.text: When it sits behind a VPN that requires MFA
When it appears only in disabled / dead code
When the affected component handles sensitive data or is reachable by unauthenticated, internet-facing users
When it is only reachable after authenticating as the data's owner

explanation: Severity is not a fixed property of a CWE — it depends on context. The SAME Open Redirect (CWE-601) is more severe when it touches sensitive data or is reachable by unauthenticated users; it is less severe behind strong compensating controls (VPN+MFA, an effective WAF rule) or when only reachable by the legitimate owner. This is exactly why CVSS has exploitability and impact dimensions rather than a single per-CWE number.
source_reference: FIRST CVSS v3.1 Specification; OWASP Risk Rating Methodology
